Subject: Interview details for Project Lead
Body:
Dear Dennis,

 

We appreciate your interest in Capita and the time you have invested in applying for a role with us.

We would like to invite you for interview so we can find out a little more about your skills and experience. As discussed, your interview has been scheduled. The details are as follows:

 

Date: 30th March 2025

Day: Monday

Time: 11:00 AM

Duration: 60 mins

Interviewer: Stuart McNaught & James Martin

Format of interview: MS Teams (see link below to join)

Interview Structure : Competency based interview  

________________________________________________________________________________

Microsoft Teams meeting

Join: https://teams.microsoft.com/meet/33105402755913?p=iFRjVaff3sDqwJ7ycD <https://teams.microsoft.com/meet/33105402755913?p=iFRjVaff3sDqwJ7ycD> 

Meeting ID: 331 054 027 559 13

Passcode: Pp7KU2QU

Join on a video conferencing device

Tenant key: capitasa@m.webex.com <mailto:capitasa@m.webex.com> 

Video ID: 129 467 982 6

More info <https://www.webex.com/msteams?confid=1294679826&tenantkey=capitasa&domain=m.webex.com> 

For organizers: Meeting options <https://teams.microsoft.com/meetingOptions/?organizerId=900b371d-2de6-4f09-a3ac-4ca5715740d1&tenantId=1edaad83-b2ef-483d-81f1-2c48682f40ec&threadId=19_meeting_Mzk2NWJmZmMtNGYwNi00MjQ0LWIxZjAtMmMzMTcyZDQ1YzJm@thread.v2&messageId=0&language=en-US>  | Reset dial-in PIN <https://dialin.teams.microsoft.com/usp/pstnconferencing> 

________________________________________________________________________________

 

During the interview, we will discuss your qualifications and experience, how you can contribute to our team and there will be some questions around the key competencies we are looking for in the successful candidate for this role.  We encourage you to prepare by reviewing the job description and researching our company. 

Please let me know if you would like to discuss any special requirements or adjustments you may need to ensure you are able to present yourself at your best.

You can confirm your availability for the interview by simply responding to this email.  Please do contact me if you have any questions or if this time is no longer suitable for you.

 

I wish you the best in your recruitment process and we are looking forward to meeting you.

 

Classification : Confidential

 

Regards,

Shraddha Suryavanshi

Subject: LYON INC — Full Company & Product Overview
Body:
Hi Dennis,
Thank you again for sharing your product exercise — I’ve reviewed it carefully, and I want to say your approach stood out.
The way you structured MVP boundaries, prioritised the workflow engine, and focused on delivery clarity is very much aligned with how we need to operate at this stage. It’s clear you understand early-stage product environments, especially the balance between speed, focus, and long-term scalability.
Before we move forward, I want to give you a complete and transparent overview of LYON INC — not just at a surface level, but in a way that gives you full context as a potential Product Manager coming into the company.

1. What LYON INC Is Building
LYON INC is being built as a unified SaaS platform for digital business operations.
At a high level, we are solving a very real and growing problem:
Tool Fragmentation
Modern businesses operate across multiple disconnected systems:
* CRM platforms
* Content scheduling tools
* Analytics dashboards
* Workflow automation systems
* Project management tools
* Communication platforms
Each tool solves a specific problem — but together they create:
* Disconnected workflows
* Data silos
* Repetitive manual processes
* Poor operational visibility
* Increased cost and inefficiency

Our Core Vision
LYON is designed to become a centralised operational system where businesses can:
* Plan, create, and schedule content
* Manage workflows and internal operations
* Automate repetitive tasks
* Track analytics and performance
* Collaborate across teams
—all within a single, unified ecosystem.

Product Direction (Important)
We are not building a collection of features.
We are building a connected system, where:
* Data flows across modules
* Actions are interconnected
* Users operate from one environment instead of multiple tools
Long-term, LYON becomes an operating layer for digital businesses, not just a tool.

2. The Core Product Philosophy
There are three principles guiding everything we build:
1. Simplicity at the Surface, Complexity Underneath
Users should feel like the system is simple — even though it handles complex workflows behind the scenes.
2. Modular but Unified
Each feature (CRM, analytics, workflows, etc.) should:
* Work independently
* But connect seamlessly into a shared system
3. Action-Oriented Experience
The platform is not just for viewing data — it’s for taking action:
* Create → Schedule → Automate → Analyse → Improve

3. Where We Are Today
We are currently in the foundational stage of the company.
What Exists
* Early prototype: https://lyoninc.net
* Initial UI/UX exploration
* High-level product direction
What Is Happening Now
* Building out the founding team
* Defining system architecture
* Structuring the MVP roadmap

4. MVP Direction (Next Phase)
The next milestone is a focused MVP build (6–8 weeks).
Core MVP Components
* Central dashboard (overview + actions)
* Content creation & scheduling
* Basic workflow/task system
* AI-assisted content generation
* Lightweight analytics

Key Principle for MVP
We are not trying to build everything.
We are validating a core value loop:
Create → Schedule → Track → Improve
Everything outside of that is secondary at this stage.

5. The Team (Current State)
We are currently operating as a lean founding team.
Current Structure
* Founder (Product direction, strategy, hiring)
* UI/UX Designer (Feman – leading design direction)
* Engineers (early-stage contributors)

How the Team Works
Because we are early-stage:
* There is no rigid hierarchy yet
* Work is highly collaborative
* Decisions are fast and iterative
* Everyone contributes beyond their role
We are building with people who:
* Think at a system level
* Take ownership
* Are comfortable with ambiguity

6. What We Are Looking to Build (Team Expansion)
To become a fully structured product team, we are actively building toward:
Immediate Priorities
* Product Manager (this role)
* Backend Developer(s)
* Additional Frontend Support
* QA / Testing (later stage)

Target Structure (Near-Term)
* Product (PM + Founder)
* Design (UI/UX Lead)
* Engineering (Frontend + Backend)
Your role would sit at the centre of this — connecting:
* Product vision
* Design execution
* Engineering delivery

7. The Role of Product Manager at LYON
This is not a “feature manager” role.
This is a foundational product leadership role.
Core Responsibilities
* Define MVP scope and priorities
* Structure product backlog
* Align design and engineering
* Drive delivery cadence
* Maintain product clarity and focus
* Make trade-off decisions under constraint

What Success Looks Like
* Clear, structured MVP
* Fast and focused execution
* Minimal wasted effort
* Strong alignment across the team

8. How We Work (Execution Model)
We aim to keep things simple but disciplined:
* Lightweight sprint cycles
* Clear priorities (no overloading)
* Direct communication (no unnecessary layers)
* Fast decision-making
Your earlier point around:
“speed and clarity — not process for its own sake”
— is exactly how we want to operate.

9. Equity Structure (Very Important)
Because we are pre-funding, the company is currently equity-based.
Equity Range
For core roles: 0.5% – 4% equity
For a Product Manager at your level: → Expected range: 2% – 4% (depending on involvement and ownership)

Vesting Structure
* 4-year vesting
* 12-month cliff
* Monthly vesting thereafter

Why Equity Matters Here
At this stage:
* There is no salary yet
* The risk is higher
* But the upside is significantly higher
You are not joining as an employee — you are joining as an early builder of the system.
If LYON succeeds:
* Early contributors hold meaningful ownership
* You benefit directly from the company’s growth

10. Compensation Transition Plan
As we progress toward:
* MVP completion
* Market validation
* Funding
We plan to transition into:
* Paid roles (salary or contract)
* While retaining equity

11. Why This Stage Is Important
This is the most impactful stage to join:
* Product is still being defined
* Systems are not locked in
* Decisions have long-term impact
You would have:
* Direct influence on product direction
* Ownership of foundational systems
* Ability to shape how the company operates

12. What We Are Looking For in You
From your exercise, you already demonstrate:
* Strong prioritisation thinking
* Clear MVP focus
* Structured execution mindset
What matters now is:
* Alignment with the vision
* Willingness to operate in early-stage conditions
* Ability to drive clarity across ambiguity

Next Steps
If this aligns with you, the next step would be:
* A deeper conversation around the role
* Alignment on time commitment
* Discussion around equity structure
* Integration into the team for MVP planning

I wanted to make this as detailed as possible so you can make an informed decision.
If you have any questions or want to explore any part further, I’m happy to go deeper.
Looking forward to your thoughts.
Best regards, 
Oladapo Owodunni
 Founder — LYON INC

Subject: Update - Granicus Application for  Program Manager
Body:
Dear Dennis, 
​
Thank you for taking the time to apply and interview for the Program Manager position at Granicus. We certainly appreciate your consideration of our company in your job search.
​
Please know that your information and qualifications have been closely reviewed by our team for this role. However, we have been lucky to receive a wealth of excellent candidates, and have decided to move forward with another candidate.
 
We are happy to keep your information readily available in the hopes of a future match. In that event, we'll be in touch with you. In the meantime, if you see another opportunity that interests you, we encourage you to apply! 
​
Thank you again for applying and for your interest in working at Granicus.
​
Sincerely,
The Granicus Recruitment Team

________________________________

Subject: M&G plc - Invite to Interview
Body:
Hello Dennis

 

I hope you are well. 

 

Thank you for taking the time to submit your application for the role of Technology Delivery Lead - FTC. 

 

We're delighted you did, as we want to know more about you and your experience. 

 

The next stage in our recruitment process is a remote interview via Microsoft Teams, which will give you the opportunity to learn more about the role and for us to learn more about you.  

 

Can you let us know which of the following times work best for you, please provide several options:

*	3/6 - 11:00 
*	4/6 - 15:00
*	5/6 - 14:00
*	8/6 - 9:30, 11:00, 14:00
*	9/6 - 4:00, 15:00
*	10/6 - 9:00, 11:00

 

Once confirmed, we will schedule this for you and provide all required details shortly after. 

 

At M&G it is our goal to ensure our interview process is accessible and fair to all applicants. If you have any questions, accommodation requests or require any adjustments prior to your interview, please do not hesitate to contact us.

 

Looking forward to hearing from you soon.

 

Kind regards,

Ciara

	
	
This email was intended for dennisnowland@virginmedia.com

Spotted Zebra invites you to complete an AI interview as part of the hiring process.

*	A device with a working camera and microphone

What is an AI interview?
An AI Interview is a live, conversational interview conducted by an AI agent. You'll be asked a series of questions relevant to the role and can respond naturally, just as you would in a real interview. The AI interviewer will engage with your answers in a back-and-forth conversation throughout.
There's no need to schedule a time, simply accept the invitation and complete the interview whenever suits you before the deadline.
Get support
You can always contact us at candidate@spottedzebra.co.uk <mailto:candidate@spottedzebra.co.uk> . 
Use of AI in this recruitment process
To support fair, consistent hiring and help identify opportunities aligned with each person's skills, AI-enabled tools may have been used during the previous stage of the recruitment process to assess relevant skills or summarise information. These tools may have helped inform decisions about your application, with appropriate human oversight and decision-making safeguards built into key stages of the recruitment process.
If you would like to ask for an additional human review, provide your point of view, or contest a decision, you can contact us at support@spottedzebra.co.uk <mailto:support@spottedzebra.co.uk> .
